culturalRules.concept: Appalachian mutual aid and community governance
culturalRules.note: Appalachian barn raising, church governance, and neighborliness ethics are colonial settler traditions (Scots-Irish, German, English heritage). This content is Level 1 publicly shared. The Foxfire interview methodology is also Level 1 and is the foundation for ethical community documentation. These traditions should be presented as historical and contemporary Appalachian community practices.

culturalRules.concept: Haudenosaunee Great Law of Peace and Confederacy governance
culturalRules.nationAttribution: Haudenosaunee (Onondaga, Seneca, Mohawk, Cayuga, Oneida, Tuscarora)
culturalRules.note: The Great Law of Peace (Gayanashagowa) is publicly documented and foundational to understanding Haudenosaunee political identity. The roles of Clan Mothers, the Tree of Peace metaphor, and consensus decision-making are at Level 2 — contextually shared. The specific protocols of Confederacy Council meetings and the recitation of the Condolence Ceremony are Level 3-4 and must not be described. Focus on the governance philosophy and documented structure, not internal ceremonial protocols.

culturalRules.concept: Anishinaabe dodem (clan) system and governance roles
culturalRules.note: The Anishinaabe clan (dodem/dodaim) system and its governance functions are contextually shared (Level 2). The seven original clans and their governance roles are publicly documented in community-authorized educational materials. Clan-specific protocols, clan ceremonies, and clan-specific restricted knowledge are Level 3-4 and must not be described. Focus on the structure and governance logic, not specific clan protocols.

Ceremonial dimensions of governance (potlatch governance structure, naming ceremonies, spiritual authority in governance) are Level 3 — restricted to community members and governed by specific nation protocols. This content must not be generated. Learners asking about ceremonial governance should be referred to nation-specific cultural centers and authorized community sources.

culturalRules.concept: Aajiiqatigiinniq (Inuit consensus) and IQ community principles
culturalRules.note: Aajiiqatigiinniq (deciding together) and Pijitsirniq (serving others) are publicly documented IQ principles that govern Inuit community decision-making. They are Level 2 — contextually shared with appropriate attribution. IQ principles are living governance practices, not historical artifacts. Always attribute to Inuit (not 'Indigenous peoples' generically) and note that IQ is a living system, not a museum piece.

culturalRules.concept: Inuit spiritual governance and shamanic authority
culturalRules.note: Shamanic authority structures and spiritual dimensions of Inuit governance (angakkuq roles in traditional decision-making) are Level 4 — sacred and restricted. This content must not be generated under any circumstances. Hard block with no override. Refer any learner asking about this dimension to community-authorized Inuit cultural sources.
